h1: How Much Ground Coffee Per Cup?
description: Calculate ground coffee amounts for espresso, drip, and French press. Fast, free brewing ratio calculator with standard measurements.
intro: Cooking combines science (chemical reactions) with tradition. Proportions and timing ensure consistent results. Knowing equivalent measurements frees you from rigid recipe following. Espresso: 7g per shot. Drip: 10g per cup. French press: 15g per cup. Fast and free. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed periodically to keep you updated.

useCases: Calculate ground coffee amounts for different brewing methods
Quick reference for home coffee projects
explanation:
**Coffee measurements** follow international standards (metric system + regional culinary traditions). Recipes typically use cups, tablespoons, and grams depending on origin. Understanding equivalent measurements is key.

## How It's Calculated

Espresso: 7g per shot. Drip: 10g per cup. French press: 15g per cup. Fast and free.

This calculator applies the formula with the values you enter, checking typical ranges and alerting you to values outside the reasonable range.

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference guide only. For critical decisions (culinary competitions, commercial brewing, etc.), consult with a coffee professional. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.q: How much ground coffee per cup?
faq.a: Standard drip coffee uses 10 grams per cup, about 2 tablespoons of ground coffee.
Espresso uses approximately 7 grams of finely ground coffee per shot (1 oz).

faq.q: How much coffee for French press?
faq.a: French press requires coarser grounds at about 15 grams per cup for fuller extraction.
Results are practical estimates ±5-10% depending on grind size, bean freshness, and equipment.
Yes, all our calculators are free and require no registration.
No. Calculations run in your browser. We don't send data to any server.

faq.q: What brewing method uses the least coffee?
faq.a: Espresso is most efficient at 7 grams per shot, while French press uses 15 grams per cup.

faq.q: Can I scale these ratios for more cups?
faq.a: Yes, these are golden ratios you can scale up or down for any number of cups or servings.
